---
title: "Clustering_ITALY"
author: "Baran Booyeh"
date: "2025-03-05"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(FactoMineR)
library(factoextra)
library(cluster)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)

italy <- read_excel("C:/Users/BARAN/Desktop/Thesis/Data Sets/ITALY/number_region2.xlsx")

df_italy <- as.data.frame(italy)

df_italy2 <- df_italy[, !(colnames(df_italy) %in% c("TOTAL"))]
rownames(df_italy2) <- df_italy[,1]
df_italy2 <- df_italy2[,-1]

df_italy2 <- df_italy2[!(rownames(df_italy2) %in% c("Apulia", "Trentino-South Tyrol")), ]

df_italy2 <- df_italy2 / rowSums(df_italy2)

ca_italy <- CA(df_italy2, graph = FALSE)

dd_italy <- dist(ca_italy$row$coord[, 1:3], method = "euclidean")

hc_ward <- hclust(dd_italy, method = "ward.D2")

plot(hc_ward, main = "Hierarchical Clustering (Ward’s Method)", xlab = "", ylab = "Height")

fviz_nbclust(ca_italy$row$coord[, 1:3], FUN = hcut, method = "wss")  

hc_clusters_2 <- cutree(hc_ward, k = 2)
hc_clusters_3 <- cutree(hc_ward, k = 3)
hc_clusters_4 <- cutree(hc_ward, k = 4)

set.seed(123)  
kmeans_2 <- kmeans(ca_italy$row$coord[, 1:3], centers = 2, nstart = 25)
kmeans_3 <- kmeans(ca_italy$row$coord[, 1:3], centers = 3, nstart = 25)
kmeans_4 <- kmeans(ca_italy$row$coord[, 1:3], centers = 4, nstart = 25)
kmeans_6 <- kmeans(ca_italy$row$coord[, 1:3], centers = 6, nstart = 25)

sil_hc_2 <- silhouette(hc_clusters_2, dd_italy)
sil_hc_3 <- silhouette(hc_clusters_3, dd_italy)
sil_hc_4 <- silhouette(hc_clusters_4, dd_italy)
sil_km_2 <- silhouette(kmeans_2$cluster, dd_italy)
sil_km_3 <- silhouette(kmeans_3$cluster, dd_italy)
sil_km_4 <- silhouette(kmeans_4$cluster, dd_italy)
sil_km_6 <- silhouette(kmeans_6$cluster, dd_italy)

silhouette_hc_2 <- mean(sil_hc_2[, 3])
silhouette_hc_3 <- mean(sil_hc_3[, 3])
silhouette_hc_4 <- mean(sil_hc_4[, 3])
silhouette_km_2 <- mean(sil_km_2[, 3])
silhouette_km_3 <- mean(sil_km_3[, 3])
silhouette_km_4 <- mean(sil_km_4[, 3])
silhouette_km_6 <- mean(sil_km_6[, 3])


cat("Silhouette Scores:\n")
cat("Hierarchical Clustering (k=2):", silhouette_hc_2, "\n")
cat("Hierarchical Clustering (k=3):", silhouette_hc_3, "\n")
cat("Hierarchical Clustering (k=4):", silhouette_hc_4, "\n")
cat("K-Means Clustering (k=2):", silhouette_km_2, "\n")
cat("K-Means Clustering (k=3):",
    silhouette_km_3, "\n")
cat("K-Means Clustering (k=4):", silhouette_km_4, "\n")
cat("K-Means Clustering (k=6):", silhouette_km_6, "\n")

fviz_silhouette(sil_hc_2) + ggtitle("Silhouette Plot - Hierarchical Clustering (k = 2)")
fviz_silhouette(sil_hc_3) + ggtitle("Silhouette Plot - Hierarchical Clustering (k = 3)")
fviz_silhouette(sil_hc_4) + ggtitle("Silhouette Plot - Hierarchical Clustering (k = 4)")
fviz_silhouette(sil_km_2) + ggtitle("Silhouette Plot - K-Means (k = 2)")
fviz_silhouette(sil_km_3) + ggtitle("Silhouette Plot - K-Means (k = 3)")
fviz_silhouette(sil_km_4) + ggtitle("Silhouette Plot - K-Means (k = 4)")
fviz_silhouette(sil_km_6) + ggtitle("Silhouette Plot - K-Means (k = 6)")


df_clusters <- data.frame(
  Region = rownames(df_italy2),
  HC_Cluster_2 = as.factor(hc_clusters_2),
  HC_Cluster_3 = as.factor(hc_clusters_3),
  HC_Cluster_4 = as.factor(hc_clusters_4),
  KMeans_Cluster_2 = as.factor(kmeans_2$cluster),
  KMeans_Cluster_3 = as.factor(kmeans_3$cluster),
  KMeans_Cluster_4 = as.factor(kmeans_4$cluster),
  KMeans_Cluster_6 = as.factor(kmeans_6$cluster)
)

df_clusters <- df_clusters %>% arrange(HC_Cluster_2)

kable(df_clusters, caption = "Comparison of Clustering Methods: Region Assignments")

```

Based on the clustering results, we determined the optimal number of clusters using the elbow method, which suggested testing k = 2 and k = 3. The silhouette scores were used to evaluate clustering quality, where higher scores indicate better-defined clusters. Overall, Hierarchical Clustering with k = 3 had the highest silhouette score, suggesting that a three-cluster model best fits the data. K-Means with k = 3 performed similarly, while k = 2 in both methods resulted in mixed regional assignments, making it less effective in capturing meaningful patterns.
